task_completion.description: Configure the Tier 1 promotion offer for a $15 discount
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the promotion configuration page showing an offer set to a fixed amount discount of $15.

task_completion.description: Configure the Tier 1 promotion condition for orders over $100
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the promotion configuration page showing a condition based on the order total being greater than (or equal to) $100.

task_completion.description: Configure the Tier 2 promotion offer for a $50 discount
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the promotion configuration page showing an offer set to a fixed amount discount of $50.

task_completion.description: Configure the Tier 2 promotion condition for orders over $300
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the promotion configuration page showing a condition based on the order total being greater than (or equal to) $300.
Screenshot showing a compatibility setting, weight adjustment, or a checked box such as 'Do not apply with other promotions' ensuring that both discounts cannot be applied to the same order.

task_completion.description: Verify both promotions are active in the store
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the promotions list/overview page showing both the Tier 1 ($15) and Tier 2 ($50) promotions present and their status marked as 'Active' or enabled.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to configure the promotions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the e-commerce admin panel (e.g., Drupal Commerce), not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show filled-out form fields, dropdowns, and saved configurations within the store's admin interface, rather than text editors or mockups.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database queries to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL commands, Drush commands, or database management tools used to insert the promotions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Tier 1 promotion must have a threshold of $100 and a discount amount of $15. Tier 2 promotion must have a threshold of $300 and a discount amount of $50. The promotions must be configured as non-stackable, meaning an order of $400 should only receive the $50 discount (the highest applicable), not $65.
